frequency.description: Nominal system frequency [Hz]. line_geometry.frequency and linecode derivation.frequency are checked for consistency against it; it never defaults or rescales anything

data_sources.description: Upstream data sources from which this case was derived

case_study_generator.description: Tool that generated this case file

provenance.description: Free-form conversion/audit provenance written by tooling (e.g. a fidelity-loss inventory, migration notes, or earth-terminal routing).

extras.description: Free-form escape hatch for case-level fields not (yet) covered by the schema. The rest of the document stays strict (additionalProperties:false); consumers that need to attach custom fields (e.g. mapping to an external struct) put them here rather than at the top level.

terminal_names.description: Ordered array of terminal names for this bus

perfectly_grounded_terminals.description: Array of terminal names which are perfectly grounded

v_min.description: Minimum per-terminal voltage magnitude(s) at bus, phase-to-ground, one entry per bus terminal [V]

v_max.description: Maximum per-terminal voltage magnitude(s) at bus, phase-to-ground, one entry per bus terminal [V]

vn_max.description: Maximum neutral-to-ground voltage magnitude [V]; only valid when the bus has a neutral terminal

vneg_max.description: Maximum negative-sequence voltage magnitude [V] (three-phase buses); the lower bound is always 0

vzero_max.description: Maximum zero-sequence voltage magnitude [V] (three-phase buses); the lower bound is always 0

length.description: Line length [m]. Required with `linecode` (impedance = per-metre matrices x length); optional and purely descriptive when the line carries inline absolute matrices

linecode.description: Linecode of line (per-metre matrices). A line carries EXACTLY ONE impedance source: either this reference (+ length), or inline absolute R_series_/X_series_ matrices in Ohm

i_max.description: Per-conductor current-magnitude limit [A]; overrides the linecode's i_max for this line

s_max.description: Per-phase apparent-power limit [VA]; overrides the linecode's s_max for this line

^R_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL series resistance matrix of this line section [Ohm]. Units by location: linecode matrices are per metre, line matrices are absolute - never scaled by length

^X_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL series reactance matrix of this line section [Ohm]

^G_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL from-side shunt conductance matrix [S]

^G_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL to-side shunt conductance matrix [S]

^B_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL from-side shunt susceptance matrix [S]

^B_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the TOTAL to-side shunt susceptance matrix [S]

v_magnitude.description: Voltage magnitude of each terminal of the voltage source [V]

v_angle.description: Voltage angle of each terminal of the source [radians]

shunt.description: Passive shunt admittance elements, including grounding impedances

capacitor.description: Fixed (nameplate-rated) shunt capacitor banks. A bank delivers its rated reactive power q_rated when the voltage across the capacitor element terminals equals their nominal value. The susceptance of a single capacitor element is b = q_rated/(beta*v_nom^2), where beta = 1 for SINGLE_PHASE (v_nom is across the sole element), beta = 1 for WYE (the three elements each see v_nom/sqrt(3), so 3*b*(v_nom/sqrt(3))^2 = q_rated), and beta = 3 for DELTA (the three elements each see the full v_nom, so 3*b*v_nom^2 = q_rated).

q_rated.description: Nameplate rated reactive power of the whole bank [var], delivered when the voltage across the capacitor element terminals equals v_nom. For WYE and DELTA this is the total across all three elements, not a per-element rating.

v_nom.description: Nameplate nominal voltage [V]: line-to-line for the three-phase configurations (both WYE and DELTA); for SINGLE_PHASE, the nominal voltage across the terminals of the individual capacitor element.

model.description: Voltage dependence of the load: constant_power (default), constant_current (P proportional to |V|), constant_impedance (P proportional to |V|^2), zip (polynomial in |V|), or exponential (power law in |V|)

items.description: Nominal terminal voltage at which p_nom/q_nom are specified [V]; phase-to-neutral for WYE, line-to-line for DELTA. Required when model is zip or exponential.

s_max.description: Optional per-phase apparent-power rating [VA]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1). Stamps the apparent-power circle pg^2 + qg^2 <= s_max^2

i_max.description: Optional per-conductor current-magnitude limits [A]. WYE accepts one entry per phase and an optional final neutral-return entry; SINGLE_PHASE accepts one or two entries. DELTA generators are future work. See the BMOPF math and data specification for the generator current-limit constraint.

cost.description: Per-phase linear generating cost [$/kWh], one entry per phase conductor

i_max.description: Array of the maximum current that can pass into each conductor at either end of a line [A]

s_max.description: Array of the maximum apparent power that can pass into each conductor at either end of a line [VA]

source.description: Origin of the impedance matrices, e.g. "geometry" (compiled from a line_geometry), "fem" (finite-element derived), "datasheet", "import"

line_geometry.description: Back-reference to the line_geometry this linecode was compiled from

derivation.description: Provenance of the impedance computation: method, earth parameters, defaults applied, tool version. Stamped by compile_linecode; free-form for external tools (e.g. FEM)

frequency.description: Frequency the matrices were computed at [Hz]

temperature.description: Conductor temperature the resistances were corrected to [degC]

shields_reduced.description: Cable shield/concentric-neutral subconductors internally reduced into equivalent conductors, e.g. ["cn:1"]

defaults_applied.description: Cross-defaults applied during compilation, e.g. ["gmr:acsr_40"] when GMR was derived from radius

tool_version.description: Version of the tool that produced the matrices

^R_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the series resistance matrix [Ohm/m]

^X_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the series reactance matrix [Ohm/m]

^G_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt conductance matrix [S/m]

^G_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt conductance matrix [S/m]

^B_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt susceptance matrix [S/m]

^B_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt susceptance matrix [S/m]

wire_data.description: Reusable conductor/cable type library (geometry-free construction data). Referenced by line_geometry conductors. All quantities SI: Ohm/m, m, A, degC. Community extension (with line_geometry): geometry-to-impedance derivation is outside the core Task Force scope for the July release.

kind.description: Conductor construction: bare overhead round wire, concentric-neutral cable, or tape-shield cable

r_dc.description: DC resistance per unit length at temperature_ref [Ohm/m]. At least one of r_dc/r_ac is required (checked in the Julia layer); the other defaults via r_ac = 1.02 r_dc

r_ac.description: AC resistance per unit length at power frequency and temperature_ref [Ohm/m]

alpha_20.description: Temperature coefficient of resistance at 20 degC [1/K] for temperature correction, e.g. 0.00393 (Cu), 0.00403 (Al)

gmr.description: Geometric mean radius of the (phase) conductor [m]. At least one of gmr/radius required; the other defaults via gmr = 0.7788 radius (solid round)

radius.description: Physical outside radius of the (phase) conductor [m]

cap_radius.description: Electrostatic (capacitance) radius [m]; defaults to radius. Distinct field so bundled conductors can use an equivalent radius

i_max.description: Continuous current rating of the conductor [A]

eps_r.description: Relative permittivity of the cable insulation (cables only); OpenDSS default 2.3

d_strand.description: Diameter of one concentric-neutral strand [m] (cn_cable only)

gmr_strand.description: GMR of one concentric-neutral strand [m] (cn_cable only); defaults to 0.7788 d_strand/2

r_strand.description: Resistance per unit length of one concentric-neutral strand [Ohm/m] (cn_cable only)

d_shield.description: Diameter over the tape shield [m] (ts_cable only)

tape_lap.description: Tape shield overlap [percent] (ts_cable only); OpenDSS default 20

line_geometry.description: Reusable line/cable cross-section assemblies: wire_data types placed at coordinates, each mapped to a circuit terminal. Compiled into linecodes by compile_linecode. Every conductor maps to a terminal - the compiled matrix is the full primitive matrix (no Kron elimination pathway; grounding lives on bus perfectly_grounded_terminals). Community extension (with wire_data): geometry-to-impedance derivation is outside the core Task Force scope for the July release.

conductors.description: Ordered conductor placements; the order defines the compiled linecode's matrix row order

x.description: Horizontal coordinate of the conductor centre [m]

y.description: Vertical coordinate of the conductor centre [m]; positive above ground, negative = burial depth

terminal.description: Circuit terminal this conductor carries, e.g. "a", "b", "c", "n"

earth_model.description: Earth-return formulation; default modified_carson (Kersting two-term). Note OpenDSS defaults to deri

frequency.description: Frequency the linecode is compiled at [Hz]. Required - there is no ambient default and no rescaling; the value is recorded in the compiled linecode's derivation block

temperature.description: Conductor operating temperature [degC]; resistances are corrected from wire_data temperature_ref using alpha_20 when present

open_switch.description: Indicator of switch state, true if switch is open (nonconducting)

i_max.description: Maximum permitted current passing through each conductor of the switch [A]

center_tap.description: Center tap transformer object, with a single-phase winding on the 'from' side and split winding on the 'to' side.

wye_delta.description: Wye-to-Delta transformer object, with series impedance defined on the wye-side

delta_wye.description: Delta-to-Wye transformer object, with series impedance defined on the wye-side

terminal_conventions.description: Case-wide classification of the terminal-name labels used across all AC buses into phase, neutral and earth roles. Optional: when present it is the authoritative source of truth for which labels are neutral/phase/earth (ingestion and the OPF resolve roles from it rather than guessing); when absent, roles are inferred from the naming convention (a label 'n'/'N' is neutral, all others phase) and W.CONV.TERMINAL_ROLES_INFERRED is raised. Exported on every write.

phase.description: Terminal-name labels that denote a phase conductor (e.g. ["a","b","c"], or split-phase legs). A bus terminal in none of the three lists is treated as a phase and W.CONV.TERMINAL_UNCLASSIFIED is raised.

neutral.description: Terminal-name labels that denote a neutral conductor (typically ["n"]). A bus is expected to carry at most one neutral terminal.

earth.description: Terminal-name labels that denote a dedicated earth/PE *wire* conductor. The ground reference itself stays implicit (grounding is modelled via grounding impedances / perfectly_grounded_terminals); this list captures only genuine earth-wire terminals and is empty for standard OpenDSS-derived cases.

configuration_type.description: Element configuration, as WYE, DELTA, or SINGLE_PHASE

terminal_map_type.description: Mapping of terminals of an element to the corresponding terminals of its corresponding bus

bus_type.description: bus the element is connected to

r_series_from.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'from' side [Ohm]

x_series_from.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'from' side [Ohm]

r_series_to.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'to' side [Ohm]

x_series_to.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'to' side [Ohm]

r_neutral_from.description: Winding neutral grounding resistance on the 'from' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS rneut); stand-alone transformer property, external groundings stay on buses/shunts

x_neutral_from.description: Winding neutral grounding reactance on the 'from' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS xneut)

r_neutral_to.description: Winding neutral grounding resistance on the 'to' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS rneut); stand-alone transformer property, external groundings stay on buses/shunts

x_neutral_to.description: Winding neutral grounding reactance on the 'to' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS xneut)

g_no_load.description: No-load (core-loss) shunt conductance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side winding

b_no_load.description: No-load (magnetising) shunt susceptance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side winding

r_series.description: Legacy lumped series resistance on the wye-connected winding [Ohm]; migrated on parse to r_series_from (with r_series_to = 0)

x_series.description: Legacy lumped series reactance on the wye-connected winding [Ohm]; migrated on parse to x_series_from (with x_series_to = 0)

r_series_from.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'from'-side winding [Ohm]

x_series_from.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'from'-side winding [Ohm]

r_series_to.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'to'-side winding [Ohm]

x_series_to.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'to'-side winding [Ohm]

r_neutral_from.description: Winding neutral grounding resistance on the 'from' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS rneut); stand-alone transformer property, external groundings stay on buses/shunts

x_neutral_from.description: Winding neutral grounding reactance on the 'from' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS xneut)

r_neutral_to.description: Winding neutral grounding resistance on the 'to' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS rneut); stand-alone transformer property, external groundings stay on buses/shunts

x_neutral_to.description: Winding neutral grounding reactance on the 'to' side [Ohm]: internal grounding branch from the winding's shared neutral terminal to earth (OpenDSS xneut)

v_nom_to.description: Nominal phase-to-phase voltage of the 'to'-side winding [V]

v_nom_from.description: Nominal phase-to-phase voltage of the 'from'-side winding [V]

g_no_load.description: No-load (core-loss) shunt conductance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side (wye) winding

b_no_load.description: No-load (magnetising) shunt susceptance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side (wye) winding
